pattern: Binary search on the count of tasks completed, then check feasibility for a given count by matching the easiest tasks against the strongest workers, spending pills only on the weakest worker in the current window who cannot otherwise reach the requirement.
hints: If some k tasks can all be completed, the k easiest tasks can also all be completed using the k strongest workers, so binary search on k and only test that specific pairing.

Within a feasibility check, walk the k chosen tasks from hardest to easiest, revealing workers strongest-first into a working window as soon as a worker (even with a pill) could reach the current requirement.

At each task, if the strongest worker revealed so far already meets it unpilled, use that worker directly; otherwise spend a pill on the weakest worker currently in the window, since every worker still in the window is guaranteed to clear the requirement once pilled.

edge_inputs.description: three tasks, one pill covers the weakest worker to complete every task
two demanding tasks, three idle workers, only one is reachable even pilled
wide spread of requirements against several equal-strength workers with multiple pills available
no pills available at all, so only raw worker strength counts
every worker needs and gets a pill to reach the requirement
a worker already strong enough for the task, so the available pill goes unused
even a full pill cannot lift any worker to the requirement, so nothing can be done
requirement and worker strength sit at the top of the allowed range, closed by exactly one pill
a single task and a single worker matching exactly, with no pill capacity at all
more workers than tasks, so the weaker extra workers are simply left unused

stress.description: randomized tasks and workers across the full value range with a random pill budget
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 1500)))
    m = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 1500)))
    cap = 1000000000
    tasks = [rng.randint(0, cap) for _ in range(n)]
    workers = [rng.randint(0, cap) for _ in range(m)]
    pills = rng.randint(0, m)
    strength = rng.randint(0, cap)
    return [tasks, workers, pills, strength]

stress.description: many workers clustered just below a shared task requirement, forcing heavy pill usage
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 800)))
    m = rng.randint(n, max(n, min(size, 800)))
    req = rng.randint(10, 1000)
    tasks = [req + rng.randint(-5, 5) for _ in range(n)]
    strength = rng.randint(1, 20)
    workers = [max(0, req - rng.randint(1, strength)) for _ in range(m)]
    pills = rng.randint(0, m)
    return [tasks, workers, pills, strength]

complexity.time: O((n + m) log(min(n, m)) log(max(n, m)))
